_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ensure security design and cybersecurity development activities are properly documented (providing a functional description of security implementation) and updated as necessary.
reference_document_element_description: Develop and maintain AI security standards for the enterprise, including training data provenance requirements, model registry controls, and LLM application security requirements.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.495), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.480).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.0/10 (raw 0.397). Shared vocabulary: [security]. DCWF-only terms [activities, cybersecurity, description, design, development, documented] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, application, controls, data, develop, enterprise] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.864, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 21-word DCWF element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Ensure security design and cybersecurity development activities are properly doc" and "Develop and maintain AI security standards for the enterprise, including trainin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.523 > STS 0.397 (gap +0.126). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [security] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.6%. Shared tokens: [security]. DCWF-only: [activities, cybersecurity, description, design, development, documented]. WIDAI-only: [ai, application, controls, data, develop, enterprise].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.126 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
